title: <$triggerAbbr>
breadcrumbs: ProcScript: Precompiler Directives and Uniface Constants
text: <$triggerAbbr> The trigger name abbreviation. <$triggerAbbr> #define <$triggerAbbr> = Value Scope Trigger or container, depending on where the constant is defined. By default, <$triggerAbbr> has container scope. Description You can explicitly define an abbreviation, or any other string, to identify a particular trigger. If you do so, you must also explicitly define the scope using #startdefine and #enddefine . For example: #startdefine #define $triggerAbbr=Detail trigger detail ... end #enddefine Copy If not explicitly defined, <$triggerAbbr> returns the following abbreviations: Table 1. Trigger and Component Name Abbreviations Abbreviation Trigger or Container Object Type ASCR USCRIPT Application shell CSCR USCRIPT Component DCLA UDECLARATIONS Application shell DCLC UDECLARATIONS Component DCLE UDECLARATIONS Entity DCLF UDECLARATIONS Field ESCR UCOLL_SCRIPT Entity FSCR USCRIPT Field OSCR UOCC_SCRIPT Entity OPTN option Menu Item PDIS preDisplay Menu If you migrated from Uniface 9, trigger abbreviations might be explicitly defined. For more information, see Migrated Trigger Abbreviations . Related Links #define Define a constant name and value. #startdefine Declares the scope of precompiler constant declarations (#define's) #enddefine Declares the end scope of precompiler constants declared after the corresponding #startdefine.
